description_long: This object is a vintage film projector, featuring a robust metal body with a combination of matte gray and black finishes. The left side displays a prominent control panel with dials, switches, and a red lever, as well as a metallic handle for carrying. The front is equipped with a large, cylindrical lens assembly, flanked by additional smaller lenses and adjustment rings, all showing signs of wear and use. The right side is more minimal, with two circular access panels and a smooth, sloped surface. The base is fitted with a sturdy, angled mounting bracket, likely for securing the projector to a tripod or stand. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed screws, textured grips, and a mix of smooth and rough metallic surfaces.
description: A vintage, industrial film projector with a metal body, prominent lens assembly, and detailed control panel. The object features a mix of gray and black finishes, visible wear, and a sturdy mounting bracket.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main lens assembly protruding outward, with a handle and adjustment knobs visible on top.
description_view_1: The left side reveals the detailed control panel with dials, a red lever, and a metallic handle, as well as the main lens and mounting bracket.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the back of the lens assembly, showing multiple lens barrels and the handle, with less visible control detail.
description_view_3: The right side is more minimal, featuring two circular access panels and a smooth, sloped surface, with the mounting bracket visible below.
attribute_lens_type: interchangeable lens with adjustment rings, large cylindrical shape, likely for film projection
attribute_control_panel_design: Located on the left side, the control panel features several dials, a red lever, and a metallic handle, with clear mechanical labeling and tactile controls.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: No visible ventilation pattern or grills are present on the exterior surfaces.
